english: worm, insect, any small vermin, esp. euphem. for louse; ˳bu-srin ('bu srin), srin-˳bu (srin 'bu), id.; ˳bu-skyógs ('bu skyogs) snail Med.; ˳bu-tágs ('bu thags) Cs., cob-web; *bu-yáṅ* (*bu yang*) (prob. a mere corruption of buṅ-ba (bung ba)) humble-bee W.; *˳bu-riṅ* (*'bu ring*) snake W.
pf. ˳bus ('bus) 1. to open, to unfold, of flowers, esp. with ḱa (kha) Pth. — 2. Cs.: to be lighted, kindled, set on fire.
Sch.: tool used in forging nails.
a coarse silky material, stated to be imported into Tibet from Nepal, and to come from some other insect than the silk-worm.
1. C., W. shoe of plaited straw. — 2. C.: *kó-wa bú-la* (*ko ba bu la*), a kind of leather, resembling chagreen.

prop.: to fall upon in a body, to rush in upon, = rúb-pa (rub pa); ćós-la ˳buṅs (chos la 'bungs) apply yourselves with might and main to religion! it is also used of one person: ˳bad ˳buṅs ('bad 'bungs) he summons all his strength, strains every nerve Dzl.

I. pf. bus (bus), ṕu(s) (phu(s)) (the latter form prob. transit., the former intransit.) fut. dbu (dbu), imp. ṕu(s) (phu(s)) 1. vb. n. to blow, lás-kyi rluṅ ˳búd-ćiṅ (las kyi rlung 'bud cing) whilst the wind of works is blowing; ćós-kyi duṅ bus (chos kyi dung bus) the trumpet of religion blew (was blown). — 2. vb. a. to blow, duṅ (dung) the trumpet; to blow away, rluṅ-gis sbúr-ma bús-pa ltar (rlung gis sbur ma bus pa ltar) like chaff blown off by the wind Dzl.; to blow up, to fan, me (me) the fire, frq.; to blow into, to inject, e. g. to apply a clyster C.; to blow or breathe upon, bsér-bus (bser bus) to be encountered by a cold wind Med.; to inflate, to distend by injecting air, lus kun bús-pa ltar skraṅs (lus kun bus pa ltar skrangs) Mng.; ˳bud-˳duṅ ('bud 'dung) Wdk. = duṅ (dung) trumpet. Cf. sbúd-pa (sbud pa) and ṕu (phu). — W. *ṕú-će* (*phu ce*). — II. pf. imp. ṕud (phud), fut. dbud (dbud) W. *ṕud-će* (*phud ce*), trs.: 1. to put off, pull off, take off C., W., the turban, hat, coat, ring etc. Glr. and elsewh.; to throw down, ṕud bźág-go (phud bzhag go) Glr., = *ṕaṅ-ste* (*phang ste*) bor (bor) W., v. sub ˳ṕéṅ-pa ('phen pa). — 2. to drive out, expel, cast out, chase away, with the accus. of the person and place, yul (yul) out of the country Tar.; yul-ṕud (yul phud) an exile Schr.; drag-pos (drag pos) by force Mil.; to let out (out of a cage); to set free, to set at liberty, to allow to pass W.; to lay out, to spend, *ṅul tsam ṕud soṅ* (*dngul tsam phud song*) how many rupees have been laid out, spent? — 3. to pull out, tear out, extract, uproot, so a tooth, C., W. — 4. to take away, to subtract, *gú-ne* (*dgu nas*) (or *gu tóg-ne* (*dgu thog nas*)) *źi ṕud-pa* (*bzhi phud pa*) (or *ṕud-na* (*phud na*)) *ṅa lus* (*lnga lus*) 4 taken from 9 leaves 5 W. — III. pf. ˳bud ('bud), vb. n. (limited perh. to W.) 1. to fall from, escape from, drop, fall down, *lág-pa-ne bud soṅ* (*lag pa nas bud song*) it escaped, dropped out of my hand; to fall off, of leaves; to fall through, *sól-wa ḍa-míg-ne bud soṅ* (*sol ba dra mig nas bud song*) the coals are fallen through the grate. — 2. to go away, to leave, e. g. to leave the service. — 3. to go out of sight, to disappear, *nyi-ma bud soṅ* (*nyi ma bud song*) the sun is gone down; *búd-kan* (*bud mkhan*) a departed (deceased) person; the ancients, those of old, pristini; to pass away, *dus-tsód bud* (*dus tshod bud*) time passes away (make haste!); *píd-ka śar-na gun bud soṅ* (*dpyid ka shar na dgun bud song*) when spring begins, winter has passed away; *bud ćug-će* (*bud bcug ce*) to cause to be lost, or to suffer to be lost, to lose.
to itch; *bun, zá-bun* (*bun, za bun*) the itch, itching W.; *bun rag* (*bun rag*) I feel an itching (B. rgyá-ba (rgya ba)).

pf. bub (bub), imp. bub(s) (bub(s)), 1. to be turned over, upside down, frq. with ḱa (kha), ḱa-˳búb-tu nyal (kha 'bub tu nyal) he lies with his face undermost; ḱa-˳bub-tu bźag (kha 'bub tu bzhag) or bor (bor) it is placed with its top lowermost, inverted, tilted, turned over; lag-˳búb (lag 'bub) (or -bubs (-bubs)) byéd-pa (byed pa) Sch.: stumbling to fall on the hands. — 2. fig., to be overthrown, destroyed, spoiled, with regard to meditation Mil.

pf. imp. ṕub(s) (phub(s)), fut. dbub (dbub), W. *ṕub-će* (*phub ce*), to put on a roof, or something for a roof; tog (thog) to make, construct a roof; gur (gur) to pitch a tent; gru-ṕúbs (gru phubs) corner-pavilion S.g.
one hundred thousand, ˳búm-tso ('bum tsho) id.; rgyai dmag ˳búm-tso lṅa (rgya'i dmag 'bum tsho lnga)
